tossups.question: A green lantern in this painting seems to be larger than its surroundings, while a flag in this painting has its three stripes inverted. One man in this painting is trying to refill his pipe from a brightly colored blue tobacco pouch, while a figure in the back of the foreground appears to be leaning limply forwards. This work brought its artist the repute that allowed him to join the group called The Wanderers. A (*) fair-haired boy in the the middle of this painting dressed in red is seen looking off intently into the distance in contrast to his comrades, who are more despondent. The main subject of this painting is a group of eleven laborers, while a ship with an unfurled sail appears further back. For 10 points, name this Ilya Repin painting showing some men dragging that ship up the namesake river.
tossups.answer: Barge Haulers on the Volga [or The Volga Boatmen]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Barge Haulers on the Volga [or The Volga Boatmen]

One man in this painting is trying to refill his pipe from a brightly colored blue tobacco pouch, while a figure in the back of the foreground appears to be leaning limply forwards. This work brought its artist the repute that allowed him to join the group called The Wanderers. A (*) fair-haired boy in the the middle of this painting dressed in red is seen looking off intently into the distance in contrast to his comrades, who are more despondent. The main subject of this painting is a group of eleven laborers, while a ship with an unfurled sail appears further back. For 10 points, name this Ilya Repin painting showing some men dragging that ship up the namesake river.

bonuses.leadin: The only person in the central group in this painting who is looking upward is a youth, the only person portrayed in bright colors. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painting in which a group of men pull a boat to shore.
Ilya Repin, the painter of Barge Haulers on the Volga, painted scenes from this country, such as a religion procession in Kursk and a depiction of Ivan the Terrible holding his dead son.

Ilya Repin belonged to this group of Russian artists who sought to depict contemporary Russian life. Isaac Levitan and Nikolai Ge belonged to this movement, which formed in rebellion against the restrictions on academic painting at the time.
answers: Barge Haulers on the Volga [or The Volga Boatmen; or Burlaki na Volge]
answers_sanitized: Barge Haulers on the Volga [or The Volga Boatmen; or Burlaki na Volge]
Russia [or Rossiya; or Russian Federation; or Rossiyskaya Federatsiya]
The Wanderers [or The Itinerants; or Peredvizhniki]

parts_sanitized: Name this painting in which a group of men pull a boat to shore.
Ilya Repin, the painter of Barge Haulers on the Volga, painted scenes from this country, such as a religion procession in Kursk and a depiction of Ivan the Terrible holding his dead son.

Ilya Repin belonged to this group of Russian artists who sought to depict contemporary Russian life. Isaac Levitan and Nikolai Ge belonged to this movement, which formed in rebellion against the restrictions on academic painting at the time.

bonuses.leadin: This painting man shows a double file line of haggard figures trawling a boat. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this painting by a member of the Wanderers.
Barge Haulers on the Volga and Religious Procession in Kursk Province are works of this painter.
Ilya Repin and the rest of the Wanderers were from this country through which the Volga flows.
